# Gmsh insane: partitioned mesh outputs

Wrap the `gmsh` CLI, generate a 3D mesh, partition it into two parts, and retain both split mesh files.

## Why This Case Exists

The top Gmsh challenge should prove more than one-shot meshing by exercising the CLI's partitioned-output path with real retained artifacts.

## Case Shape

- ID: `gmsh_insane`
- Family: `gmsh`
- Difficulty: `insane`
- Tier: `local_full`
- Style: `command`
- Workflow Slice: `aerodynamics`
- Target Kind: `command`
- Upstream Target: `gmsh`
- Catalog Source: `challenges/cases/gmsh_insane/challenge.toml`

## Ingestion Breadth

This case relies on its workflow without extra breadth assertions.

## Why This Stays A Challenge

This case keeps its ingestion recipe and workflow assertions in
`challenge.toml` instead of checking in companion `ingest.py` and
`use.py` scripts. That boundary is intentional: the challenge lane
should stay a compact problem statement rather than turning into a
worked answer key like the case-study lane.

## Run This Case

Use the convenience target:

```bash
make challenge CASE=gmsh_insane
```

Or call the runner directly:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python scripts/run_challenges.py --catalog-dir challenges/cases --tier all --only gmsh_insane
```

## Prepared Inputs

This case does not need rendered setup inputs.

## Fixtures

- `challenges/cases/gmsh_insane/fixtures/box.geo`

## Workflow

### 1. Generate and partition the tiny box mesh into two split outputs

Tool: `run_gmsh`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "cwd": "{challenge_artifact_dir}",
  "extra_argv": [
    "{challenge_fixture_dir}/box.geo",
    "-3",
    "-part",
    "2",
    "-part_split",
    "-save_topology",
    "-o",
    "{challenge_artifact_dir}/partitioned.msh"
  ]
}
```

Expectations: json fields {'exit_code': 0}; files are non-empty ['{challenge_artifact_dir}/partitioned_1.msh', '{challenge_artifact_dir}/partitioned_2.msh'].

## What This Case Proves

- A deterministic wrapper can drive richer meshing options than a single default solve.
- The challenge harness can retain multiple real partitioned mesh outputs from one CLI workflow.
- A mesh-generation CLI can be pressure-tested on more than one useful output path without custom adapters.

## Challenge Definition

```toml
# Self-contained case: push Gmsh through mesh generation and partitioned writeback.
# This top rung keeps the geometry tiny while producing multiple real partition outputs.
id = "gmsh_insane"
title = "Gmsh insane: partitioned mesh outputs"
tier = "local_full"
style = "command"
slice = "aerodynamics"
family = "gmsh"
difficulty = "insane"

[example]
summary = "Wrap the `gmsh` CLI, generate a 3D mesh, partition it into two parts, and retain both split mesh files."
motivation = "The top Gmsh challenge should prove more than one-shot meshing by exercising the CLI's partitioned-output path with real retained artifacts."
proves = [
  "A deterministic wrapper can drive richer meshing options than a single default solve.",
  "The challenge harness can retain multiple real partitioned mesh outputs from one CLI workflow.",
  "A mesh-generation CLI can be pressure-tested on more than one useful output path without custom adapters.",
]

[target]
kind = "command"
value = ["gmsh"]

[probe]
commands = [["gmsh"]]

[scaffold]
kind = "command"
function_name = "run_gmsh"
help_probe_args = ["-help"]

[workflow]
[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Generate and partition the tiny box mesh into two split outputs"
tool = "run_gmsh"
arguments = { extra_argv = ["{challenge_fixture_dir}/box.geo", "-3", "-part", "2", "-part_split", "-save_topology", "-o", "{challenge_artifact_dir}/partitioned.msh"], cwd = "{challenge_artifact_dir}" }

[workflow.steps.expect]
json_fields = { exit_code = 0 }
files_nonempty = [
  "{challenge_artifact_dir}/partitioned_1.msh",
  "{challenge_artifact_dir}/partitioned_2.msh",
]
```
